target.name: Bedrock Robotics
target.founder: Boris Sofman, Kevin Peterson, Ajay Gummalla, Tom Eliaz

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $1.8B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $1.6B.

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: The Bot Company
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B)

private.name: Kinetic Automation
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
Bedrock Robotics, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Boris Sofman, Kevin Peterson, Ajay Gummalla, and Tom Eliaz, is evaluated against a comparable universe of 9 private companies and 1 public company. The company carries a current post-money valuation of $1.75B following $270M in total capital raised.

Two valuation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage advancement to the $1.8B last post-money value, implying a valuation of $3.3B. The peer-median method examines three same-sector and same/adjacent-stage comparables—Dexterity (Series B, $1.6B), Hadrian (Series C, $1.6B), and Nimble Robotics (Series C, $1.0B)—yielding a median of $1.6B with a range of $1.0B to $1.6B.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $1.4B (low) to $3.8B (high), with a median of $2.5B. The stage-progression method suggests upside potential, while the peer-median approach anchors closer to the current valuation, reflecting observable market pricing for similar-stage robotics businesses. Key uncertainties include the tightness of the peer comparables around Series B-to-C transition economics and the degree to which Bedrock's specific operational metrics and market position align with the identified peer set.
